'''Pita Godinet''' (born 21 December 1987) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Dapto Canaries]] in the [[Illawarra Rugby League]].

==Background==
Godinet was born in [[Christchurch]], New Zealand.{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Early years==
Godinet is a Sydenham and [[Richmond Rovers]] junior.<ref>[http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profiles-display&id=2589&team=Auckland+Vulcans+%28NC%29 Pita Godinet] ''NSWRL''</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.voxy.co.nz/sport/godinet-and-houma-secure-nrl-contracts/5/83842|title=Godinet and Houma secure NRL contracts|date=26 February 2011|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Godinet played for the [[Waitakere Rangers]] in the [[2007 Bartercard Cup]].<ref name=pro>[http://www.prosportplayaz.co.nz/PITA_GODINET.php Pita Godinet] ''prosportplayaz.co.nz''</ref>

In 2008 he represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] in the [[Bartercard Premiership]], and played for the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[NSW Cup]]. He was named the Vulcans rookie of the year.<ref name=pro/>
===Te Atatu Roosters===
In 2010 Godinet signed with the [[Te Atatu Roosters]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/aucklander/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=1503376&objectid=11000955|title=Auckland gulps up Shore league|work=The Aucklander/New Zealand Herald|date=26 August 2010|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref> In 2010 he represented Auckland in the [[Albert Baskerville Trophy]], and played for the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[NSW Cup]]. He was selected at halfback in the NSW Cup team of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/pita-godinet-named-in-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year|title=Pita Godinet named in NSW Cup Team of the Year|website=leagueunlimited.com|date=9 September 2010|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref>
===New Zealand Warriors===
In October 2010 Godinet was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] to take part in their 2010-2011 pre-season training.<ref>[http://www.voxy.co.nz/sport/nzrl-upgrades-challenge-trophy-first-ever-kiwis-samoa-test/5/67598 NZRL Upgrades Challenge Trophy For First Ever Kiwis-Samoa Test] ''Voxy.co.nz'', 15 October 2010.</ref> This was updated to a full-time contract in February 2011.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10709115|title=NRL: Warriors sign two local youngsters|date=27 February 2011|agency=[[NZPA]]|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref> He was also part of the [[Manurewa Marlins]] in 2011.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/auckland/local-news/manukau-courier/opinion/blog-at-sixes-and-sevens|title=Local league wrap-up|author=Mackey, Turei|date=29 April 2011|work=[[Western Leader]]|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref> He has re-signed with the Warriors until the end of 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=39635|title=Lillyman one of eight Warriors to extend contracts|website=rleague.com|date=29 May 2011|access-date=29 May 2011|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120908100746/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=39635|archive-date=8 September 2012}}</ref>

Godinet was named to make his [[National Rugby League]] début on 24 July 2011, replacing an injured [[Lance Hohaia]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10740282&ref=rss|title=NRL: Moon rising in team's centre stakes|author=Deane, Steve|date=23 July 2011|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref> Godinet had a "dream debut", scoring a try in his first match.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10740741&ref=rss|title=NRL: Dream début for Godinet|date=25 July 2011|agency=[[NZPA]]|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref>
===Manly Sea Eagles===
During the 2016 pre-season, Godinet trialled with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], and was subsequently signed to a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/02/13/all-stars-trial-games-team-lists/|title=All Stars, trial games team lists|publisher=NRL|date=13 February 2016|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/off-the-record-rugby-league-transfer-gossip-23/|title=Off the Record: Rugby League Transfer Gossip #23|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|date=17 February 2016|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-market-watch-bulldogs-massive-coup-titans-eye-wrecking-ball-manly-sign-halfback/news-story/b647203311d85fa1713a517c3a7b7468|title=NRL Market Watch: Bulldogs' massive coup, Titans eye wrecking ball, Manly sign halfback|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref>

Godinet featured for the Manly Sea Eagles in the [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines]], and signed for the Wests Tigers in December 2017.<ref name="Media">{{cite news|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2017/12/04/wests-tigers-sign-pita-godinet/|title=Wests Tigers sign Pita Godinet|publisher=NRL Digital Media|work=NRL CLUBS|date=4 December 2017|language=en-au|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/02/04/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/|title=2017 Downer NRL Auckland Nines squads|publisher=NRL|date=4 February 2017|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref><ref name=smh/>

===Atlanta Rhinos===
On 11 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Atlanta Rhinos]] in the [[North American Rugby League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 May 2021|accessdate=12 May 2021|title=From France to the USA: Samoa international Pita Godinet joins Atlanta Rhinos|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/from-france-to-the-usa-samoa-international-pita-godinet-joins-atlanta-rhinos/}}</ref>
==Representative career==
Godinet made his début for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] on 16 October 2010, being called into the side to replace the injured [[Ben Roberts (rugby league)|Ben Roberts]] at halfback. Godinet scored the only try for Samoa.<ref>[https://www.sky.co.nz/sport?tabid=55&cid=32342 Kiwis thump Samoa] ''SkySport.co.nz'', 16 October 2010. Retrieved 27 March 2018.</ref>

In 2013, Godinet was selected to play for the Samoans in their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign. He appeared in all four of the Samoans' games in the Tournament. He scored one try against the [[France national rugby league team|French]] in the group stage.

On 7 October 2014, Godinet was selected in the [[Samoa national rugby league team]] 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] series.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2014/10/07/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|publisher=NRL|date=7 October 2014|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad - Zero Tackle|date=7 October 2014|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref>

In 2016 Godinet played for [[American Samoa national rugby league team|Amerika Samoa]] in the [[Cabramatta International Nines]] tournament.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.radionz.co.nz/international/pacific-news/295227/sport-former-toa-samoa-star-pita-godinet-switches-allegiance|title=Former Toa Samoa star Pita Godinet switches allegiance|publisher=Radio New Zealand|date=29 January 2016|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref> On 7 May 2016, Godinet returned to playing for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], playing in the [[International rugby league in 2016#Samoa vs Tonga men in Australia|2016 Polynesian Cup]] against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] where he played as the halfback in the 18–6 win at [[Parramatta Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/05/07/samoa-holds-on-in-pulsating-pacific-test/|title=Samoa holds on in pulsating Pacific Test|publisher=NRL|date=7 May 2016|access-date=27 March 2018}}</ref> Later in the year, he was also part of the Samoan team that took on Fiji in a historical [[2016 Samoa vs. Fiji rugby league test match|test match]] in Apia.
